conditions: Mild anxiety related to major life transition
medications: Cetirizine 10 mg as needed during allergy season
health.mental_health: Mostly stable but has intermittent anxiety and sleep disruption tied to planning an early retirement and uncertainty about long-term healthcare coverage; uses breathing exercises and occasional therapy check-ins.
recent_visits: Pulmonology follow-up at Massachusetts General Hospital to review asthma control and update inhaler plan (late January)
Annual physical with primary care in Back Bay; discussed sleep issues and stress management (early February)
Optometry visit in Cambridge for new prescription after increased screen time (December)

finance.debt: Carried about $18,000 in credit card and personal loan balance from an uneven year of client work; aggressively paying it down while building a cash buffer for retirement.
investments: Solo 401(k) with a mix of S&P 500 index fund and total bond market fund
I Bonds purchased during high-inflation period
recent_transactions: Transferred $6,500 to Roth IRA contribution for the year (January)
Paid $1,200 to credit card toward debt snowball (February)
Purchased a used Mac Studio and color-calibrated monitor for $2,900 (December)
Paid $1,050 for COBRA bridge coverage deposit to avoid a gap while planning post-retirement insurance options (February)

relationships: Partner: Elena Rossi (lives together in Boston; supportive but cautious about early retirement timeline)
Younger sister: Talia Mitchell (lives in Providence; frequent calls about family planning and finances)
Close friend: Devon Clarke (former coworker; informal accountability buddy for retirement prep)
Mentor: Priya Nair (creative director contact who refers him higher-paying branding work)
social.social_media_activity: Uses Instagram and Behance to post curated portfolio work and process clips; keeps personal posts minimal. On LinkedIn, he’s been quietly networking with other freelancers and small agencies as a backup plan in case retirement gets delayed.
private_conversations: Discussing with Elena whether to postpone having kids because of the early retirement plan and healthcare uncertainty
Admitting to Devon that he’s worried his asthma could make private insurance more expensive or complicated post-retirement
Talking with Priya about stepping away from client work without burning bridges and how to frame it publicly

schedule.work_hours: Typically 25–35 billable hours/week, mostly weekdays 10:00am–5:00pm, with occasional evening deadlines during launch weeks.
regular_appointments: Therapy check-in (telehealth) every other Thursday at 6:30pm
Asthma maintenance review/peak-flow tracking monthly (self-check) and inhaler refill reminders
Weekly budgeting/retirement planning session on Sundays (1 hour) with shared spreadsheet
upcoming_events: Meeting with fee-only financial planner in the Financial District to stress-test early retirement plan (next Tuesday 3:00pm)
Client brand presentation for a Somerville coffee roaster rebrand (Friday 11:00am)
Weekend trip to Portland, Maine with Elena as a low-cost “pre-retirement” trial getaway (late March)
schedule.daily_routine: Starts with a short walk on the Emerald Necklace when weather allows, then deep work blocks for design and client comms, mid-afternoon gym or home workout, and evenings split between cooking, portfolio maintenance, and retirement planning tasks.

professional.company: Independent studio operating under the name 'Mitchell Type & Color' (sole proprietor; works with Boston-area startups and nonprofits)
projects: Full brand identity and packaging system for a Somerville coffee roaster (logo, label set, brand guidelines)
Pitch deck redesign for a Seaport-based health tech startup seeking Series A
Event collateral and signage for a Cambridge nonprofit gala (print + digital assets)
Website refresh (Figma + handoff) for a boutique fitness studio in Brookline
professional.performance_issues: Has been turning down long retainer contracts and pushing for shorter scopes to align with retiring within 1–2 years, which sometimes strains client expectations and creates gaps between projects.
workplace_relationships: Relies on a long-term freelance copywriter (Jules Hernandez) for brand voice work; discussing how to transition referrals after he steps back
Occasional subcontracting from a small agency in South End; he hasn’t disclosed his retirement timeline to avoid losing steady overflow work
Print vendor relationship with a local shop in Allston; negotiates rush fees carefully due to tighter cash-flow planning

hobbies: Street photography around Beacon Hill and the Harborwalk
Sketching type studies and poster design for fun
Board game nights with a small friend group
habits: Tracks spending weekly and runs a monthly 'retirement readiness' checklist
Uses an air purifier at home and checks pollen forecasts to prevent asthma flare-ups
Limits alcohol most weeks but tends to stress-snack late at night during heavy planning periods
Keeps a detailed personal document with account logins, insurance notes, and an emergency plan for his partner
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a one-bedroom apartment in Boston (near Fenway/Kenmore area) with his partner; considering downsizing to a cheaper neighborhood or moving to a lower-cost New England town once he stops taking on steady client work.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly Mediterranean-leaning home cooking (fish, vegetables, whole grains); avoids heavy dairy on high-pollen days because it feels harder to breathe; moderate caffeine due to occasional anxiety.
